title: V-ing / p.p. 作形容詞修飾名詞

formulas.zh: 現在分詞修飾名詞，表示名詞正在進行的動作或主動性質
過去分詞修飾名詞，表示名詞所承受的動作或完成的狀態

explanation: 分詞作形容詞修飾名詞時，單個分詞通常前置於名詞，分詞片語則後置修飾。V-ing表主動或進行中的動作（如 a running boy 正在跑步的男孩），p.p.表被動或已完成的動作（如 a broken window 破損的窗戶）。此句型常見於學測閱讀與寫作，須注意分詞的邏輯主語與名詞的一致性，區分前置單詞與後置片語的位置差異。

examples.eng: The sleeping child looked peaceful and innocent in the warm sunlight.
examples.chi: 那個正在睡覺的孩子在溫暖陽光下看起來平靜而無辜。

examples.eng: The abandoned dog found in the shelter was adopted by a kind family yesterday.
examples.chi: 在收容所發現的被遺棄狗狗昨天被一個善心家庭領養了。

examples.eng: Students studying for the exam realized that time management was crucial for success.
examples.chi: 正在為考試準備的學生們意識到時間管理對成功至關重要。

examples.eng: The damaged buildings caused by the earthquake were carefully reconstructed over five years.
examples.chi: 地震造成的受損建築物在五年內被仔細重建。

extension.tip: 區分主被動：V-ing表名詞主動執行動作（a reading student 正在讀書的學生），p.p.表名詞被動接受動作（a read book 被讀過的書）。判斷時問「名詞是否主動做這件事」，若是用V-ing，若被動接受則用p.p.。注意片語長度決定位置：簡短用前置，複雜用後置。

examples.test: a crying baby vs. a cried baby
examples.result: → crying baby正確（嬰兒在哭）；cried baby不自然，p.p.用於如a beaten baby（被毆打的嬰兒）等被動狀態

examples.test: a walking man vs. a walked path
examples.result: → walking man正確（男人正在走路）；walked path不常用，應用walked-on path或well-trodden path表示被踩過的小徑

examples.test: students studying hard vs. problems solved carefully
examples.result: → 前者V-ing片語後置（學生正努力讀書），後者p.p.片語後置（問題被仔細解決），兩者邏輯主語與名詞一致

extension.note: 記住：V-ing=主動進行中，p.p.=被動已完成，分詞位置看複雜度，短詞前置長片語後置。

parts: girl waved goodbye to her family at the airport.
exercises.sentence: The departing girl waved goodbye to her family at the airport.
exercises.translation: 那個正在離開的女孩在機場向她的家人揮手告別。

parts: during the archaeological dig was carefully preserved in the museum.
exercises.sentence: The ancient vase discovered during the archaeological dig was carefully preserved in the museum.
exercises.translation: 在考古挖掘中被發現的古代花瓶在博物館裡被小心保護。

parts: children played happily in the park all afternoon.
exercises.sentence: The laughing children played happily in the park all afternoon.
exercises.translation: 那些正在笑的孩子們整個下午都在公園裡開心地玩耍。

parts: in the fire were considered lost forever until someone recovered them.
exercises.sentence: The documents burned in the fire were considered lost forever until someone recovered them.
exercises.translation: 在火災中被燒毀的文件被認為永久丟失了，直到有人發現它們。

vocab_table.zh: 令人興奮的、激動人心的
